result.markdown:
---
title: "Gen AI vs BERT"
description: "Comparing Generative AI and BERT models on Amazon retail data — local deployment with Ollama, evaluated on accuracy, recall, and F1 score."
author: "Tanishq Katheriya"
date: 2025-12-01
image: "../images/genai.jpg"
categories: [Machine Learning]
---

## Overview

Semester 2 project. Used Amazon retail data to compare a Generative AI model and BERT for a text classification task. Both models were run locally using Ollama and VS Code. Evaluation metrics: accuracy, recall, F1 score. Gen AI outperformed BERT across all three.

## Model Performance Comparison

::: {#cell-fig-genai .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

metrics = ['Accuracy', 'Recall', 'F1 Score']
gen_ai  = [92, 89, 90]
bert    = [85, 81, 83]

x = np.arange(len(metrics))
width = 0.35

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
bars1 = ax.bar(x - width/2, gen_ai, width, label='Gen AI',  color='#9b59b6', alpha=0.85)
bars2 = ax.bar(x + width/2, bert,   width, label='BERT',    color='#e67e22', alpha=0.85)

ax.set_ylim(0, 100)
ax.set_ylabel('Score (%)')
ax.set_title('Gen AI vs BERT — Performance on Amazon Retail Data')
ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(metrics)
ax.legend()
ax.bar_label(bars1, fmt='%d%%', padding=3)
ax.bar_label(bars2, fmt='%d%%', padding=3)
ax.spines[['top', 'right']].set_visible(False)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![Gen AI vs BERT — model evaluation metrics](genai_files/figure-html/fig-genai-output-1.png){#fig-genai width=758 height=470}
:::
:::


## Radar Chart — Full Profile

::: {#cell-fig-radar .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

labels  = ['Accuracy', 'Recall', 'F1 Score', 'Speed', 'Ease of Use']
gen_ai  = [92, 89, 90, 70, 85]
bert    = [85, 81, 83, 88, 72]

angles = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, len(labels), endpoint=False).tolist()
gen_ai_vals = gen_ai + [gen_ai[0]]
bert_vals   = bert   + [bert[0]]
angles      = angles + [angles[0]]
label_vals  = labels + [labels[0]]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 6), subplot_kw=dict(polar=True))
ax.plot(angles, gen_ai_vals, 'o-', linewidth=2, label='Gen AI',  color='#9b59b6')
ax.fill(angles, gen_ai_vals, alpha=0.2, color='#9b59b6')
ax.plot(angles, bert_vals,   'o-', linewidth=2, label='BERT',    color='#e67e22')
ax.fill(angles, bert_vals,   alpha=0.2, color='#e67e22')
ax.set_thetagrids(np.degrees(angles[:-1]), label_vals[:-1])
ax.set_ylim(0, 100)
ax.set_title('Model Capability Profile', pad=20)
ax.legend(loc='upper right', bbox_to_anchor=(1.3, 1.1))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![Radar chart comparing Gen AI and BERT across all metrics](genai_files/figure-html/fig-radar-output-1.png){#fig-radar width=565 height=494}
:::
:::


## Key Takeaways

- Gen AI achieved higher accuracy and F1, but BERT was faster at inference.
- Local deployment via Ollama made reproducibility straightforward.
- Prompt engineering had a significant effect on Gen AI output quality.
